audit_method: Direct URL fetch via WebFetch (markdown extraction). 2026-06-18 rows: each source URL was fetched directly; no WebSearch snippets were used for published claims; Garfield County Housing Authority 2024-report figures are marked 'partial' (model extraction, re-confirm verbatim). 2026-06-19 additions (regional-modular-pipeline, silt-recent-planning): the curator re-confirmed web sources s6/s7/s8/s9/s12 by direct WebFetch on 2026-06-19 (Habitat RFV modular page, Post Independent x2, EVstudio architect page, leg.colorado.gov SB25-002). The two Town of Silt P&Z packet PDFs (s10 Feb-3, s11 Apr-7) were fetched and text-extracted directly from the source PDFs by a research agent and are marked 'partial' pending verbatim re-confirmation against the packets. 2026-06-20 addition (official-affordability-discussion): the s13 Feb-9 Board of Trustees minutes (a scanned, text-layer-less PDF) were read first-hand by the curator via the PDF reader, so the two s13 rows are 'supported' with verbatim quotes; the Heron's Nest applicant-affordability framing (s10) and the Riverview no-condition finding (s11) are 'partial', from a research agent's direct PDF text-extraction pending verbatim re-confirmation.

rows.supporting_quote: 20 Project Based Voucher apartment building consisting of 16 one bedroom and 4 studio units ... 701 Home Ave in Silt, CO ... Managed by the Garfield County Housing Authority ... open to any low-income Senior (62+) and Disabled (55+) residents. Residency preference: must currently live, work, or be hired to work in Garfield County.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via WebFetch.

rows.supporting_quote: In 2027, project-based vouchers at Silt Senior Housing will sunset, due to the 40-year HUD limit.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via WebFetch; the only Silt-specific item in the 2024 GCHA report.

rows.supporting_quote: 240 affordable housing units managed by the authority; 35 deed-restricted rentals; approximately 559 county families assisted monthly through mainstream, housing choice, and emergency housing voucher programs; $4,476,468 paid to local landlords through HUD rental assistance.
rows.notes: Figures captured via WebFetch extraction of the report; curator should re-confirm exact counts verbatim.

rows.supporting_quote: $77,400 in new interest-free down-payment assistance loans provided in 2024; 16 deed-restricted units sold in 2024; No new HUD vouchers will be issued until year-end due to a $700,000 recapture by HUD; 7 project-based vouchers to Benedict (Glenwood Springs, 2025); 14 for Rifle Apartments (2025); 8 projected for Aster Place (Parachute, 2026-27); 20 projected for Canyon Vista (Glenwood Springs, 2026-27).
rows.notes: Figures captured via WebFetch extraction; pipeline locations confirm none are in Silt. Curator should re-confirm exact counts verbatim.

rows.supporting_quote: Priority One: Applicants that are employed full-time by a Garfield County-based employment source; Priority Two: full-time residents of Garfield County; Priority Three: Applicants who will become full-time residents ... Full-time is defined as a minimum of 32 hours per week ... four Category Income levels ... ranging from Category One at 80% AMI Income thru Category 4 at 120% AMI Income.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via WebFetch.

rows.supporting_quote: Four communities feature deed restrictions with a maximum resale price with a 3% annual appreciation based on the Consumer Price Index for Garfield County: Midland Point (near Carbondale), Blue Creek Ranch, Valley View West (West Glenwood), Ironbridge (outside Glenwood Springs).
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via WebFetch; none of the four are in Silt.

rows.supporting_quote: Subdivision Name: Mountain View. Location: Approximately two miles north of Silt on a 27-acre rural zone district property. Five lots ranging from 3.9 to 6.8 acres each. Developer: Red Dog LLC. Approval Date: Monday, November 19, 2025. Single-family houses ... with the option to add an accessory dwelling unit. No deed-restricted or affordable housing is mentioned in the article.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via WebFetch.

rows.supporting_quote: Promote a range of attainable housing choices ... Promote residential opportunities downtown and maintain downtown vitality ... Promote infill/redevelopment which should be the first priority for future growth.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via WebFetch; plan framework language. Exact adoption year not asserted (page history ambiguous).

rows.supporting_quote: located at 2515 Centennial Pkwy on U.S. Highway 6 [Rifle Energy Innovation Center] ... net-zero, all electric, sustainable modular construction.
rows.notes: Re-confirmed by direct WebFetch on 2026-06-19. Confirms Rifle location and net-zero/all-electric; the 200-homes figure is sourced to s7, not this page.

rows.supporting_quote: Habitat for Humanity is in the process of developing a $16 million facility here in Rifle that will, ultimately, within the next several years, be producing up to 200 homes (units) a year and hiring 64 employees and training up to 100 students a year at the facility.
rows.notes: Re-confirmed by direct WebFetch on 2026-06-19. Quote attributed to Gail Schwartz, President of Habitat for Humanity of the RFV.

rows.supporting_quote: The 63,000-square-foot Modular Production and Advanced Manufacturing Training Facility ... measures 210 by 300 feet ... net-zero, all-electric, and sustainable practices.
rows.notes: Re-confirmed by direct WebFetch on 2026-06-19. Architect's design figure is 63,000 sq ft (one news account rounded to 66,000); the brief flags the discrepancy.

rows.supporting_quote: partnership with the Colorado River BOCES and Colorado Mountain College (CMC) to train and certify a local workforce ... educating up to 100 high school and post-graduate students per year.
rows.notes: Re-confirmed by direct WebFetch on 2026-06-19.

rows.supporting_quote: training up to 100 students a year at the facility.
rows.notes: Re-confirmed by direct WebFetch on 2026-06-19. Corroborates the 100-students figure from s6.

rows.supporting_quote: the state plumbing board, the state electrical board, and the state fire suppression administrator do not have jurisdiction over and their rules do not apply to a factory-built structure ... [the state housing board must adopt the regional building code standards] by July 1, 2026.
rows.notes: Re-confirmed by direct WebFetch on 2026-06-19. Bill title: 'Regional Building Codes for Factory-Built Structures' (2025 Regular Session) — corrects the user-supplied 'SB 2' to SB25-002 and 'local' to state-board jurisdiction.

rows.supporting_quote: Heron's Nest – Major Subdivision Sketch Plan ... Heron's Nest – Planned Unit Development Continuation (agenda). A full-text read of the early-2026 packets returns no LIHTC / low-income-housing-tax-credit item.
rows.notes: Figures captured via direct PDF fetch + text extraction by a research agent on 2026-06-19; curator should re-confirm verbatim against the packet. 'No LIHTC' is confirmed by absence across the Feb/Mar/Apr 2026 packets.

rows.supporting_quote: This is an application to annex 27.468 acres known as the Heron's Nest parcel ... adjoins the Town of Silt municipal boundary on the Southwest ... The project will be developed in phases, with the first phase expected to begin in 2-3 years, pending financing and permits ... Minimum unit size (square feet) 650.
rows.notes: Figures captured via direct PDF fetch + text extraction by a research agent on 2026-06-19; curator should re-confirm verbatim against the packet.

rows.supporting_quote: The Applicant proposes to replace the Ferguson Crossing PUD with a new Riverview PUD ... 14.95-acre ... the proposed residential density is 198 multi-family units and the commercial square footage is approximately 28,550.
rows.notes: Figures captured via direct PDF fetch + text extraction by a research agent on 2026-06-19; curator should re-confirm verbatim against the April-7 packet.

rows.supporting_quote: All six Garfield County municipalities, New Castle, Rifle, Silt, Parachute, Glenwood Springs and Carbondale, had previously opted in ... Garfield County must add approximately 41 affordable housing units per year for the next two years.
rows.notes: Re-confirmed by direct WebFetch on 2026-06-19. The user-supplied 'Housing Opportunity Goal report, Dec 31 2026' framing was NOT in this article and the DOLA page returned HTTP 403, so the brief states only the verified opt-in and the countywide ~41-unit/yr target.

rows.supporting_quote: There was additional discussion on the phasing of the project, the bike trail, affordable housing and its definition, a potential path to the north side of the interstate, the trail system, mailboxes and sidewalks. ... [public comment] it is not going to help their property values with the potential lower income housing ... [Mike Styk] asked how it was going to benefit the citizens of town. ... Trustee Classen made a motion to approve Resolution No. 7 ... the motion carried unanimously.
rows.notes: Read FIRST-HAND by the curator from the scanned Feb 9, 2026 BOT minutes PDF via the PDF reader (4 pages; quote from page 2). Resolution No. 7 (Heron's Nest sketch plan) passed unanimously with no affordability condition attached.

rows.supporting_quote: The Affordable Housing District includes 98 single-family attractive and affordable homes to moderate income residents. ... The applicant has worked with the Town of Silt on grant options to obtain funds to assist with the affordability of the housing units.
rows.notes: Applicant framing captured via direct PDF fetch + text extraction by a research agent from the Feb-3 P&Z packet; curator to re-confirm verbatim. Affordability is grant-dependent, not a deed restriction or an adopted condition.

rows.supporting_quote: There would also need to be consideration of whether there would be a break given to affordable housing developments.
rows.notes: Read FIRST-HAND by the curator from the Feb 9, 2026 BOT minutes (page 3, Facilities Assessment / impact-fee discussion).

rows.supporting_quote: (Absence finding: a full-text search of the March-3 and April-7 Riverview preliminary-plan packets returns no 'affordable', 'attainable', 'AMI', 'inclusionary', or 'deed-restricted' project condition.)
rows.notes: Absence-of-condition finding from a research agent's full-text search of the Riverview packets on 2026-06-19; curator to re-confirm. Marked 'partial' because it asserts the absence of a term rather than quoting a positive statement.
